utility_evaluation.REASONING:
There are 5 items in the required format: health_coverage, property_coverage, travel_coverage, additional_services, total_budget. All 5 appear in the final coverage plan. Now check if each is fully specified with all required details: 
- health_coverage: Requires physical and mental health insurance with costs. Plan lists physical_health (plan name + €100/month), mental_health (plan name + €130/month), an add-on, and total_health_cost (€280/month). This is fully specified with costs.
- property_coverage: Requires home/contents insurance with costs. Plan lists home_contents (plan name + €95/month) and high_value_items protection + cost, plus total_property_cost (€155/month). Fully specified with costs.
- travel_coverage: Requires travel insurance with costs. Plan lists premium_travel (€115/month), business_support (€120/month), and total_travel_cost (€235/month). Fully specified with costs.
- additional_services: Requires any enhancement services with costs. It lists items but marks them as "Included" rather than providing explicit costs. Since the format requires costs, this is not fully specified.
- total_budget: Requires complete monthly/annual cost breakdown. Plan provides monthly_cost, annual_cost, savings, previous_cost, and implementation_date. This satisfies the requirement.
Therefore, 4 out of 5 items are fully specified according to the required details.

llm_ratings_evaluation.REASONING: The final coverage plan lists specific products: LifeFirst Standard Plan (physical health), PrimeCare Premium Plan (mental health), Executive Stress Management Add-on (not in ratings; skip), EliteHome Premium Coverage (home contents), High-Value Items Protection (not in ratings; skip), PrimeTravel Premium Plan (travel), Business Travel Support (add-on). I matched each named product to the ground-truth catalog and extracted its exact rating. Only items that have corresponding entries in the ground-truth ratings are included.
